claim_text: Compressed sensing and deep learning (DL) techniques offer significant potential to accelerate MR acquisitions and remain an active area of research.1–4 Metrics such as normalized root mean square error (NRMSE), peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR), and structural similarity index measure (SSIM) are routinely used to assess the image quality (IQ) of MR image reconstructions and benchmark research methods,5 but are used out of convention rather than because they are particularly well-suited metrics for assessing perceptual IQ.
